01_initial_request.input_data: Labeled training images of seen classes and semantic information (attributes/text) for seen and unseen classes
01_initial_request.user_intent: To provide a comprehensive survey and categorization of zero-shot learning methods based on semantic space construction
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Classification accuracy on unseen object classes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Zero-shot learning (ZSL) requires semantic information to recognize unseen classes. [Gap] Semantic space construction methods vary and need systematic explanation. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive survey, chose to use Semantic Space Construction Analysis to perform review of engineered and learned spaces on semantic information, expecting to obtain understanding of bridge between seen and unseen classes.

parameters.space_types: engineered semantic spaces, learned semantic spaces

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Semantic space identified as important part of ZSL acting as a bridge between seen and unseen classes containing information about both.

references.relevance: Categorizes semantic spaces into engineered and learned types.
Discusses label embedding and attribute matrix generation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Various ZSL methods exist using different prior sources of knowledge. [Gap] Existing surveys do not focus on categorization based on used linguistic information. [Decision] To achieve specialized classification, chose to use ZSL Method Categorization to perform classification of methods on prior source of knowledge, expecting to obtain four distinct categories.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Methods classified into Attribute-based (A_ZSL), Text-based (T_ZSL), Latent attribute-based (Lt_ZSL), and Integrated (I_ZSL).

references.relevance: Example of Latent attribute-based ZSL method.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ZSL methods require datasets for training and evaluation. [Gap] Need to identify predominant databases used for ZSL. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive survey, chose to use Dataset Analysis to perform review of ZSL databases on image and attribute counts, expecting to obtain list of key datasets.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified Animal with Attributes (AWA), AWA2, Caltech UCSD Birds (CUB), Attribute Pascal and Yahoo (aPY), and ImageNet as predominant databases.

references.relevance: Introduced AWA2 dataset and proposed splits.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Performance of ZSL models needs measurement. [Gap] Accuracy metric alone is not enough for imbalanced datasets. [Decision] To achieve accurate evaluation, chose to use Performance Evaluation Definition to perform specification of metrics on model performance, expecting to obtain standard evaluation measures.

parameters.metrics: top-1 accuracy, top-T accuracy, F1 score

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Established Top-1/Top-T accuracy for small/large scale datasets and F1 score for imbalanced data as performance evaluation measures.

references.relevance: Reference for F1 score calculation equation.
Reference for dataset splits and evaluation settings.

papers_reviewed.interpretation: Comprehensive review of more than 100 papers related to ZSL.

categories_defined.interpretation: Four distinct ZSL method categories defined based on semantic space construction.

datasets_analyzed.interpretation: Five predominant databases (AWA, AWA2, CUB, aPY, ImageNet) detailed for training and evaluation.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective to provide a comprehensive survey and categorization of zero-shot learning methods based on semantic space construction was achieved. The paper successfully reviewed over 100 papers, categorized methods into four types, detailed key datasets, and defined performance evaluation measures.
